note: Tempdoc 629 #5 — the governed register for the store-recoverability classification. The Java authority is StoreCatalog; this register mirrors it (the gate enforces PARITY so neither can drift). It is promoted from the #1 descriptor list now that THREE obligations read the class (encryption cipher-selection, encrypted backup/export, export-first-uninstall). The gate also enforces NO-HARDCODE: every storeCipher(...) call in a construction site must read the catalog (StoreCatalog.X.recoverability()), never a bare StoreRecoverability literal — so a new store cannot get a cipher without a catalog entry, which is what makes 'a new unclassified store fails the build' true. Conforms to the readiness-reason-codes / execution-surfaces register+gate pattern; not a parallel governance mechanism.
